questions.text: When it comes to vehicles — do you actually own a car, or are you fully relying on rideshares, rentals, and public transit wherever you go? And if you do own one, what's the setup — is it something you keep in the US, in Mexico, or do you just rent when you need wheels?

What's your relationship with podcasts — are you someone who listens to them regularly (commute, workouts, cooking), or is it more of an occasional thing? Any specific shows or genres you keep coming back to?

For languages — are you actively working on improving your Spanish, or is it more of a 'get by with basics' situation? Do you prefer diving into the local culture to learn naturally, or do you like structured study (apps, classes, tutors)?

How do you handle unexpected disruptions to your routine — things like flight cancellations, sudden move plans, health scares, or plans falling through? Are you flexible and go-with-the-flow, or do you have specific coping strategies for when life throws curveballs?

When it comes to political and civic engagement — do you have strong opinions on how you participate (voting, local issues, community involvement), or is that something you mostly stay out of? How do you think about your role as a citizen wherever you're living?
